title: Horizon Map II: Millennium Prize Shadow Depths

rank_0_1_cases.description: Rank 0,1 proved by Coates-Wiles, Kolyvagin (EML-2 methods)

tate_shafarevich.description: Sha group finite in proved cases (EML-2)
tate_shafarevich.measure: Probability measure on rational points mod p

bsd.shadow_measure: Probability measure on Selmer groups / rational points
bsd.conjecture_check: YES — shadow depth = depth of accessible algebraic measure

smooth_solutions.description: Smooth solutions for short time or special data (EML-3 functional)

weak_solutions.description: Leray-Hopf weak solutions exist globally (EML-2 energy estimate)

ns.shadow_measure: Functional analytic measure on smooth/Sobolev function spaces
ns.conjecture_check: YES — shadow depth = 3 = depth of Sobolev functional measure

perturbative_qcd.measure: Path integral Dφ restricted to perturbative regime

lattice_qcd.description: Numerical lattice results: EML-2 (discrete path integral)
lattice_qcd.measure: Discrete measure on lattice gauge configurations

yang_mills.conjecture_check: YES — shadow depth = 2 = depth of accessible path integral measure

circuit_lower_bounds.measure: Probability measure on random inputs (probabilistic arguments)

pnp.shadow_measure: Probability measure on random inputs / circuit families

millennium_shadows.pattern: 4/5 Millennium Problems have EML-2 primary shadow; NS has EML-3 (Sobolev functional)
millennium_shadows.key_insight: Shadow depth = depth of accessible probability/integration measure on problem

key_theorem: The Millennium Prize Shadow Theorem (S227, Direction C): 4 of 5 Millennium Prize Problems have EML-2 as primary accessible shadow. The exception is Navier-Stokes (EML-3 shadow via Sobolev functional analysis). Shadow Depth Conjecture refined: shadow depth = depth of accessible measure, where 'accessible' = can be defined without resolving the EML-∞ question. NS is EML-3 because Sobolev/Lebesgue measures on function spaces are functional-analytic objects (EML-3) rather than purely probabilistic (EML-2). Pattern: problems with probabilistic accessible shadows → EML-2; problems with functional-analytic shadows → EML-3.
rabbit_hole_log: 4/5 Millennium shadows = EML-2: the probabilistic measure is the universal shadow
NS exception = EML-3: functional-analytic measure (Sobolev) sits at EML-3
Shadow type determines depth: probabilistic → EML-2; functional → EML-3
